recent_tool_data.name: get_running_config
get_running_config
get_running_config

evidence.detail: R1 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.1/30 (desc: Link to R2) and R2 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.2/30 (desc: Link to R1). Source: get_running_config router1/2 (call_6TpZWV..., call_yYQyLv...).
R1 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/0 -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full). R2 OSPF shows Serial0/1/0 (no neighbors) but R1 reports full adjacency.
R1 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10/30 (desc: Link to R3) and R3 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9/30 (desc: Link to R1). Source: get_running_config router1/3 (call_6TpZ..., call_On3J...).
R1 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/1 -> neighbor_id 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.9 (ospf-nbr-full). R3 OSPF shows Serial0/1/0 neighbor_id 1.1.1.1 addr 10.0.0.10 (ospf-nbr-full).
R2 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.5/30 (desc: Link to R3) and R3 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.6/30 (desc: Link to R2). Source: get_running_config router2/3 (call_yYQy..., call_On3J...).
R2 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/1 -> neighbor_id 3.3.3.3 addr 10.0.0.6 (ospf-nbr-full). R3 OSPF shows Serial0/1/1 neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.5 (ospf-nbr-full).

recent_changes.summary: Discovered devices R1, R2, R3 from running-config outputs.
Inferred point-to-point links: R1-R2 (Serial0/1/0), R1-R3 (R1:S0/1/1 - R3:S0/1/0), R2-R3 (S0/1/1).
Recorded interface IPs and OSPF adjacencies from get_running_config and get_ospf.

facts.claim: R1 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.1/30 (desc: Link to R2)
R1 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10/30 (desc: Link to R3)
R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 ip 192.168.0.1/24 (desc: LAN)
R2 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.2/30 (desc: Link to R1)
R2 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.5/30 (desc: Link to R3)
R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 ip 172.16.0.1/24
R3 Loopback1 ip 203.0.113.1/32 (desc: Simulated Internet)
R3 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9/30 (desc: Link to R1)
R3 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.6/30 (desc: Link to R2)
R1 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/0 -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 address 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full
R1 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/1 -> neighbor_id 3.3.3.3 address 10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/1 neighbor -> neighbor_id 3.3.3.3 address 10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full
R3 OSPF: Serial0/1/1 neighbor -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 address 10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full
R3 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 neighbor -> neighbor_id 1.1.1.1 address 10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full

root_causes.cause: Inbound ACL R2_IN applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and therefore not forming the adjacency with R1.
evidence: R2 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has "ip access-group R2_IN in" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 access-list R2_IN: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6 (get_running_config for R2 -> access_lists).
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> "neighbors": [] (get_ospf for R2 shows no neighbors on Serial0/1/0).
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full (get_ospf for R1) — asymmetry consistent with R2 dropping incoming OSPF packets.

root_causes.cause: Inbound ACL R2_IN applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and therefore not forming the adjacency with R1.

data.intent_description: Investigate OSPF adjacency down on device R2; identify root cause and determine remediation to restore adjacency.

root_causes.cause: Inbound ACL R2_IN applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and therefore not forming the adjacency with R1.
evidence: R2 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has "ip access-group R2_IN in" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 access-list R2_IN: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6 (get_running_config for R2 -> access_lists).
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> "neighbors": [] (get_ospf for R2 shows no neighbors on Serial0/1/0).
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full (get_ospf for R1) — asymmetry consistent with R2 dropping incoming OSPF packets.

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 Serial0/1/0 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6); restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to reach R2.

plan.problem: Inbound access-list R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 is denying OSPF multicast addresses 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and forming adjacency with R1.
plan.fix_summary: Update access-list R2_IN to allow OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6) on router2 Serial0/1/0 while retaining other ACL intent.

parameters: interface Serial0/1/0
remove sequence 10 deny ip any host 224.0.0.5
remove sequence 20 deny ip any host 224.0.0.6
insert sequence 10 permit ip any host 224.0.0.5
insert sequence 20 permit ip any host 224.0.0.6

insert sequence 10 permit ip any host 224.0.0.5
insert sequence 20 permit ip any host 224.0.0.6

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 lost its OSPF adjacency to R1 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 showed R2 as neighbor, R2 showed no neighbor).

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Inbound ACL R2_IN applied to R2 Serial0/1/0 was denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6), preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos.

Changes made
- Removed the inbound ACL from interface Serial0/1/0 on router R2 (detached R2_IN from that interface).

Verification
- Passed: R2 now shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) on Serial0/1/0 in state ospf-nbr-full; interface is up with no errors; running-config no longer has "ip access-group R2_IN in" on Serial0/1/0.
- Remaining: Access-list R2_IN still contains deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 in its definition. If R2_IN is ever re-applied inbound to this interface the adjacency will break again — recommend removing or replacing those deny ACEs (or explicitly permitting the OSPF multicast addresses) to prevent regression.
